etymology.description: 止(止) (zhǐ, "foot") + 儿(儿) (ér, "person") = foot in front of person -> to go forward -> first, previous
etymology.interpretation: The character 先(xiān, "first; previous") combines 止(止) (zhǐ, "foot") and 儿(儿) (ér, "person") to depict a foot moving forward in front of a person, symbolizing the act of proceeding or being ahead, leading to the meaning of 'first' as something that goes before or precedes others.

examples.chinese: 我先去商店(我先去商店)。
examples.english: I will go to the store first.

examples.chinese: 你需要先完成作业(你需要先完成作業)，然后才能看电视(然後才能看電視)。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ xūyào xiān wánchéng zuòyè, ránhòu cáinéng kàn diànshì.
examples.english: You need to finish your homework first, then you can watch TV.

examples.chinese: 请先听我说(請先聽我說)，再决定(再決定)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng xiān tīng wǒ shuō, zài juédìng.
examples.english: Please listen to me first before making a decision.
